result:
Here's a summary of all the changes made to implement the "Toggle Auto Advance" feature:

## Files Modified (7 files)

### 1. `AnkiDroid/src/main/res/values/11-arrays.xml`
- Added string resource `gesture_toggle_auto_advance` ("Toggle Auto Advance")

### 2. `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/cardviewer/ViewerCommand.kt`
- Added `TOGGLE_AUTO_ADVANCE` enum entry with string resource reference
- Added default keyboard binding: **Shift+A** (matching Anki Desktop's shortcut)

### 3. `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/ui/windows/reviewer/autoadvance/AutoAdvance.kt`
- Added `isEnabledFlow: MutableStateFlow<Boolean>` (starts as `true`)
- Added `toggle()` method that flips the enabled state and cancels pending jobs when disabling
- Added `isEnabledFlow.value` checks at the start of `onShowQuestion()` and `onShowAnswer()` — when disabled, these methods return early without scheduling any timers

### 4. `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/ui/windows/reviewer/ReviewerViewModel.kt`
- Added `toggleAutoAdvance()` method that delegates to `autoAdvance.toggle()`
- Exposed `isAutoAdvanceEnabledFlow` from the `autoAdvance` instance for UI observation

### 5. `AnkiDroid/src/main/res/menu/reviewer2.xml` (new reviewer)
- Added `action_toggle_auto_advance` menu item

### 6. `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/ui/windows/reviewer/ReviewerFragment.kt` (new reviewer)
- Added handler for `R.id.action_toggle_auto_advance` → `viewModel.toggleAutoAdvance()`

### 7. `AnkiDroid/src/main/res/menu/reviewer.xml` (old reviewer)
- Added `action_toggle_auto_advance` menu item

### 8. `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/Reviewer.kt` (old reviewer)
- Added handler for `R.id.action_toggle_auto_advance` → `executeCommand(ViewerCommand.TOGGLE_AUTO_ADVANCE)`

### 9. `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/AbstractFlashcardViewer.kt` (base class)
- Added `TOGGLE_AUTO_ADVANCE` case in `executeCommand()` that toggles `automaticAnswer` enable/disable (for the old reviewer path)

## How it works

- **New Reviewer (ReviewerFragment)**: The `AutoAdvance` class now has a `MutableStateFlow<Boolean>` (`isEnabledFlow`) that controls whether auto-advance timers are scheduled. When toggled off, any pending timer jobs are cancelled and new timers won't be started.
- **Old Reviewer (Reviewer.kt)**: Uses the existing `AutomaticAnswer.enable()`/`disable()` methods through the `executeCommand` mechanism.
- **Keyboard shortcut**: Shift+A (matches Anki Desktop's shortcut for the same feature)
- **Menu item**: Available in the overflow menu of both old and new reviewer
